title: PostgreSQL 18 Schema, Constraints, Keys, and Query-Driven Indexes · Contract Lens: Feed Ts Index Evidence
summary: The schema stores nested parser objects as JSON, URLs are mutable keys, tenant relationships are unchecked, text fields are unlimited, and indexes have no query evidence. Predict the reader decision, external-unknown boundary, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL transaction, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed ts index evidence increment in constrained TypeScript feed-service relational model. Learners progres…

Encode required fields, lengths, checks, states, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, and temporal rules in PostgreSQL.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Pair B-tree, partial, expression, covering, and GIN indexes with actual query shapes, selectivity, write cost, plans, and removal criteria.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Index Evidence model · Theory Feed Ts Index Evid…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Index Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Index Evidence model · Theory Feed Ts Index Evid…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 4325 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile relational entities, stable keys, constraints, tenant scope, and query-driven indexes; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, migrations, lockfile, package, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one emitted artifact, package, container, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Pair B-tree, partial, expression, covering, and GIN indexes with actual query shapes, selectivity, write cost, plans, and removal criteria.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Index Evidence model · Theory Feed Ts Index Evid…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Adding an index to every filter column is harmless.
